notes: 3.5% peak participation sufficiency threshold for nonviolent campaign success across 323 campaigns 1900-2006
5% threshold for collective action derived from rational choice framework
~10% committed minority on complete graphs (binary naming game)
~4% committed minority on sparse Erdos-Renyi random networks
~15% committed minority on dense scale-free networks
25% committed minority needed to overturn established social convention in online experiment
0.3% threshold for social contagion with higher-order interactions on hypergraphs
16% cumulative adoption (innovators + early adopters) marks inflection point; meta-analysis of 5200+ diffusion studies
Cascade window boundary: global cascades occur when individual threshold phi < ~0.18 on random networks with intermediate connectivity
Individual preference threshold of 1/3 same-type neighbors produces complete spatial segregation from random initial conditions
~10% committed minority threshold on coevolving (adaptive rewiring) networks; consistent with Xie et al. despite network dynamics
Experimental social tipping: societies tipped at TT-23 (5/6) and TT-30 (6/6) conditions; model predicts ~25% threshold. 54 experimental societies of 20 individuals
Segregation tipping point in Chicago 1970s: ~5% minority share triggers white population outflows. Regression discontinuity on Census data
Average segregation tipping point across major US cities 1970-2000: ~13% minority share
Segregation tipping point in more racially tolerant cities: ~20% minority share
Critical seed fraction for global cascades on Erdos-Renyi networks (phi=0.18 z~6): ~3.5% seed fraction triggers discontinuous cascade
During times of crisis/ambiguity committed minority threshold drops well below 10% to ~5% (Cooperative Decision Making Model)
Analytical derivation: committed minority threshold increases to ~12% on sparse ER graphs vs ~10% on complete graphs

Social influence threshold for climate-friendly behaviors: below ~30% perceived peer adoption the social influence effect is negative; above it becomes positive. Survey of ~30000 across 23 European countries
Original empirical observation of white flight tipping: ~20% minority composition triggers rapid neighborhood turnover in Chicago
Meta-analysis of 95 observations from 39 studies: average modeling threshold ~24%. Range 10-43%
Meta-analysis of 95 observations from 39 studies: average empirical threshold ~27%. Modal value ~25%
~10% committed minority on Barabasi-Albert scale-free networks; threshold robust across topologies
Network-based microfoundation of Granovetter model: social tipping emerges as saddle-node bifurcation; threshold ~10% on ER networks with moderate connectivity
Baseline (no crisis) committed minority threshold in Cooperative Decision Making Model: ~10% consistent with naming game results
Complex contagion on clustered networks: behavior spreads to 54% of population when ~14% initial seeds present in clustered lattice topology
EV adoption tipping point: once EV sales reach ~5% of new car sales adoption enters exponential growth. Empirical analysis across countries
Leipzig Monday demonstrations: peak mobilization ~3M of ~16M GDR population (~19-20%) before regime collapse in Oct 1989. Informational cascade model
Critical mass theory: with accelerating returns ~5% of highly resourceful group members can provide collective goods that trigger broader participation
With single committed minority (no competition) threshold remains ~10% on complete graphs; phase boundary emerges with two competing committed groups
